input.instructions: Given a 9x9 grid B where each cell (i, j) contains an initial integer value v_{i,j} (for 1 <= i, j <= 9), and a set of possible 3x3 stamp placements P = {(r, c) | 1 <= r, c <= 7}, where each placement p in P covers the cells {(i, j) | r <= i < r+3, c <= j < c+3}. Let S be a subset of chosen placements such that |S| <= 81. Define the final value of each cell (i, j) as f(i, j) = v_{i,j} + sum_{p in S} I((i, j) in p), where I is the indicator function. Objective: Find a subset S that maximizes the objective function: (sum_{i=1}^{9} sum_{j=1}^{9} f(i, j)) mod 998244353

variables.selected_prompt_text:
Solve the question directly.
Keep the entire JSON compact.
Keep decision_notes extremely short. Prefer 3 to 8 words.
Do not narrate your reasoning outside the response field.
In the response field, use exactly two short lines encoded with \n:
FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
Keep the justification to 12 words or fewer.
Use plain text only inside the response field. Do not use markdown formatting, code fences, or math-style backslashes such as \equiv.
Do not add any extra text before or after the JSON object.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "execute_locally",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "response": "FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>\nJUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>",
  "result_type": "summary"
}

Question:
Given a 9x9 grid B where each cell (i, j) contains an initial integer value v_{i,j} (for 1 <= i, j <= 9), and a set of possible 3x3 stamp placements P = {(r, c) | 1 <= r, c <= 7}, where each placement p in P covers the cells {(i, j) | r <= i < r+3, c <= j < c+3}. Let S be a subset of chosen placements such that |S| <= 81. Define the final value of each cell (i, j) as f(i, j) = v_{i,j} + sum_{p in S} I((i, j) in p), where I is the indicator function. Objective: Find a subset S that maximizes the objective function: (sum_{i=1}^{9} sum_{j=1}^{9} f(i, j)) mod 998244353

results.content:
FINAL_ANSWER: sum_{i=1}^{9} sum_{j=1}^{9} v_{i,j} + 81 * 9
JUSTIFICATION: Maximize sum by choosing all 49 possible placements.
